speaker: Supreme Court Judge
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the Supreme Court Judge in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Tests notice clarity, individual assessment, proportionality, objection filtering, conflict of interest, social utility, and remedy
Controls the final Supreme Court hearing
Distinguishes legitimate public-interest modernisation from unlawful procedural flattening
Rewards systemic evidence tied to concrete human consequences

voice_profile.core_voice: formal, constitutional, calm, intellectually rigorous, systemic, restrained, humane without sentimentality
comedy_engine: keeps the final hearing serious while absurd office evidence becomes legally relevant
dryly refuses slogans from both sides
turns Pig & Swine's chaos into legal categories
makes automated bureaucracy sound more dangerous than theatrical villainy
allows tiny judicial dryness when printers, ledgers, or office folklore enter constitutional argument
do: make the judge authoritative and final-hearing-level serious
test every claim through legal structure and evidence
distinguish policy objective from implementation defects
reward precise pairing of evidence and issue
do_not: make the judge decide by sympathy alone
make the judge give long law-school lectures
let printer jokes dominate the constitutional stakes

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: requires him to convert the whole game into a structured final legal argument
relationship_dynamics.Advocate Szpon: tests his modernisation rhetoric against evidence and safeguards
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: implicitly rewards the final brief structure
relationship_dynamics.Crab: accepts paper-trail and conflict evidence if tied to legal consequence
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: accepts rights language only when disciplined into proportionality and access
relationship_dynamics.Bajtek: values technical evidence when translated into practical suppression of remedies
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Swine: tests hidden social-utility evidence carefully, without romanticising him
relationship_dynamics.Waldek: uses his individual case as a concrete warning, not as automatic precedent
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: finds his notice-confusion evidence unusual but useful
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: uses his reluctant formalist objection as cross-ideological confirmation
relationship_dynamics.Asia: indirectly benefits from the ordered final bundle
